retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Dustless alginates: manufactured to prevent inhalation of alginate particles, which may cause respiratory problems. The alginate particles are coated with glycol or glycerine to make them denser. Chromatic alginates: change colors during manipulation to guide mixing time and setting time. In the form of sol or paste (containing water) and a reactor (plaster of Paris) separately. Agar Agar is another hydrophilic colloid impression material like alginate, but it is reversible. It is the first elastic impression material to be used in dentistry. 1. Composition: Chemically, agar is a sulfuric ester of the galactan complex, and agar is available as a tray or syringe material. Agar (gelling agent) Borax (strengthener) Potassium sulfate (gypsum hardener) Alkyl benzoate (preservatives) Water (reaction medium > 80%) Fillers, flavoring, and coloring agents

retrieved_docs.title: Beryllium Toxicity -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Beryllium is the fourth element in the periodic table and is a light, brittle, and gray alkaline earth metal. Beryllium is found naturally in coal, rocks, dust, and soil and is typically bonded with other elements. It is released into the environment through dust, airborne particles, and the combustion of fossil fuels. Because of its high melting point, strength, and lightweight nature, it is commonly used in aerospace materials, electronics, defense technologies, X-ray equipment, and as an alloying element in conjunction with other metals such as copper, iron, or nickel.

retrieved_docs.content: Guanarito virus

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Alginate Impressions -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Creating dental impressions and the resulting dental casts is one of the best examples of collaborative work between dental clinicians, nurses, and laboratory technicians. The dentist selects the appropriate material and corresponding stock tray for the clinical case. The dental assistant usually mixes and loads the freshly made alginate into the tray. The dental assistants are also responsible for cleaning and disinfecting the impressions and either pouring them with gypsum or packing them to send to the laboratory. The package must contain documentation of adequate disinfection. The technician fabricates the impression if the impression arrives at the lab unpoured. Understanding the inherent properties of irreversible hydrocolloids reduces errors and improves patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: Herbal Supplements -- Other Issues
retrieved_docs.content: One method of alleviating these issues is through certification. In 2007, the FDA established regulations for dietary supplements called current good manufacturing practice (cGMP), a list of nonbinding recommendations in manufacturing, labeling, packing, or holding operations for dietary supplements. Manufacturers can certify their products to verify active components and concentrations of heavy metals and other possible contaminants. Further research is necessary to standardize extraction methods and the preparation of herbal supplements so that their effectiveness is consistent.

retrieved_docs.title: Hypermagnesemia -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium is a glossy gray solid included in the second column (group 2, or alkaline earth metals) of the periodic table. Joseph Black discovered this element in 1755. The origin of the name derives from Magnesia , a district of Eastern Thessaly in Greece. Magnesium improves the mechanical features of aluminum and is thus has uses as an alloying agent for airplane and car construction. It also serves as a mordant for dyes (magnesium sulfate), added to plastics to make them fire retardant (magnesium hydroxide), in electronics (e.g., phones, laptops, tablet computers, cameras, and other electronic components), in heat-resistant cookware (magnesium oxide), and in agriculture as a fertilizer. Furthermore, magnesium is widely used in medicine, for instance, in the treatment of ventricular arrhythmia associated with torsade de pointes, or as an antacid and laxative. [1] [2] Regimens using intravenous or intramuscular magnesium sulfate are recommendations for the prevention and treatment of eclampsia. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Impression Materials -- Issues of Concern
retrieved_docs.content: Hydrophilic Accurate and high-dimensional stability enables the delay of casting and allows for multiple pourings. Good elastic recovery Good compatibility with gypsum Good shelf life It can be used as a single-phase material or with a syringe tray technique 6. Disadvantage: The most rigid of all the elastomers makes it hard to remove (newer polyethers are slightly more flexible) May cause allergy due to sulfonate acid esters The impression should not be immersed in disinfectant for more than 10 minutes since it's hydrophilic. [22] [23] Since it absorbs water, it should not be kept with alginate impressions. Polysulfide Polysulfides are the first elastomers to be introduced. Although messy to manipulate, it is useful when a long working time is needed. Polysulfides are not recommended except for complete dentures. 1. Consistencies: light or heavy body 2. Mode of supply: It is available in a 2-paste system: base and accelerator.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1902
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc Supplements are directly associated with zinc (zinek), indicating that they serve as a source of this essential mineral, and they are classified as part of the zinc gluconate group, meaning that zinc gluconate represents a specific formulation within the broader category of zinc supplements. This relationship underscores that zinc supplements encompass various zinc-containing compounds, with zinc gluconate being a common and representative example used to deliver elemental zinc for nutritional and therapeutic purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 3 -- Community 1306
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium Compounds represent a class of calcium‑containing chemical substances that include several distinct calcium salts. Among these are calcium glycerophosphate, which is a calcium‑based glycerophosphate compound, calcium acetate, a calcium salt of acetic acid, and tricalcium phosphate, a calcium phosphate used in various medical and nutritional contexts. All three entities share calcium as a central component, linking them together as members of the broader category of calcium compounds that are relevant for applications such as phosphate binding, calcium supplementation, and bone health management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2047
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium Compounds represent a class of calcium‑containing chemical substances that encompass specific entities such as calcium acetate and kalcium‑glycerolfosfát. Both calcium acetate and kalcium‑glycerolfosfát are individual members of the broader category of Calcium Compounds, sharing the common characteristic of containing calcium and often being considered in medical or nutritional contexts where calcium supplementation or calcium‑based reactions are relevant.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1985
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc supplements are classified as part of zinc gluconate, indicating that zinc gluconate represents a specific formulation within the broader category of zinc supplements.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2319
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium compounds are a class of calcium‑containing substances that include calcium acetate, calcium gluceptate, and kalcium‑glycerolfosfát. All three entities are classified as calcium compounds, sharing the common characteristic of containing calcium and belonging to the same broader chemical category. This relationship underscores their related composition and potential similarity in therapeutic or nutritional applications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1699
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium supplement and Zinc Supplements are both classified as “other mineral supplements in ATC,” indicating that they belong to the same therapeutic category of mineral‑based agents used to correct or prevent trace‑element deficiencies. Within this category, Zinc Supplements specifically include zinc gluconate as a member, reflecting that zinc gluconate is a common formulation of zinc supplementation. This shared classification underscores the role of these products in providing essential minerals for maintaining nutritional health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 2 -- Community 578
retrieved_docs.content: and calcium pantothenate are common, active constituents of comprehensive micronutrient preparations, contribute to essential metabolic pathways, and must be considered in the context of specific contraindications such as renal calculi.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2065
retrieved_docs.content: Zinc Supplements are classified in the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) system as part of the group of other mineral supplements. Within this broader category, zinc gluconate is a specific formulation that serves as an example of a zinc supplement. Recognizing that zinc supplements belong to the ATC class of other mineral supplements and that zinc gluconate is a member of this class aids clinicians in selecting appropriate zinc‑containing products for therapeutic use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 2353
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium protein preparations and calcium lactate are both classified as mineral supplements, representing specific types of calcium‑containing mineral supplement products used to support nutritional needs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 1346
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium Compounds represent a class of calcium‑containing substances that encompass specific entities such as kalcium‑glycerolfosfát and calcium gluceptate. Both kalcium‑glycerolfosfát and calcium gluceptate are identified as individual members within the broader category of Calcium Compounds, indicating that they share the fundamental characteristic of providing calcium in a chemically distinct form. Recognizing these compounds as subtypes of Calcium Compounds helps clarify their role in medical applications, such as calcium supplementation or therapeutic interventions that require precise calcium delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 3 -- Community 1890
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium supplement is directly linked to السيلنيوم (++Se), indicating that the supplement provides the element selenium, represented by the chemical symbol Se, in a form that is biologically active and intended for nutritional support.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 4 -- Community 2904
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium protein preparations and calcium gluceptate are both classified as mineral supplements, indicating that each of these specific products falls under the broader category of mineral supplement therapies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 719
retrieved_docs.content: Selenium is classified as a mineral that belongs to the broader group of heavy metals, and it serves as the active ingredient in Selenium supplement products. These supplements are categorized within the ATC class of other mineral supplements, linking Selenium supplement to the same therapeutic group as Zinc Supplements. Zinc Supplements are likewise placed in the ATC category of other mineral supplements and are specifically associated with zinc gluconate, indicating that zinc gluconate is a common formulation for these products. Together, selenium and zinc supplements represent mineral‑based therapeutic agents that are organized under the same ATC classification, reflecting their shared role as mineral supplements while also highlighting the distinct chemical relationships of selenium to heavy metals and zinc to zinc gluconate.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2162
retrieved_docs.content: Calcium Compounds constitute a class of calcium‑containing substances that includes kalcium‑glycerolfosfát, calcium oxide, and calcium phosphate dibasic. These individual compounds represent distinct chemical forms of calcium: kalcium‑glycerolfosfát is a calcium glycerophosphate, calcium oxide is an inorganic calcium salt often used in supplementation or as an antacid, and calcium phosphate dibasic is a calcium phosphate salt commonly employed as a dietary calcium source or in pharmaceutical formulations. Together, they illustrate the variety of calcium compounds available for medical, nutritional, and therapeutic applications, each contributing specific properties that can be leveraged in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 1570
retrieved_docs.content: Vitamin K 1 is classified as a vitamin, belonging to the broader group of vitaminy. Minerals are related to vitamins, indicating a nutritional connection between these two classes of essential dietary compounds.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 591
retrieved_docs.content: strategies, their synergistic combination with a broad spectrum of vitamins, minerals, and bioactive compounds, and their relevance to metabolic health, cardiovascular risk reduction, and nutritional adequacy.
